rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH DICK VS. LA GALLERIA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, A NON
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-22-600034 WITH CASE CPF-22-517780
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 18, 2022 line 1. PETITIONER JOSEPH DICK MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-22-600034 WITH CASE CPF-22-517780 is GRANTED. Actions are consolidated for all purposed. 517780 shall be the lead case. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: IVONNE J. MARTINEZ ET AL VS. ROJEL CARRANZA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Extend The Five Year Statute Of Ccp Section 583.310 Pursuant To Ccp Section 583.34
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 18, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFF IVONNE MARTINEZ, MARIA MARTINEZ Notice Of Motion And Motion To Extend The Five Year Statute Of Ccp Section 583.310 Pursuant To Ccp Section 583.34 is DENIED. The delay caused by COVID-19 extended the time to bring the case to trial by 6 months. It was not "impossible, impracticable, or futile" to bring this case to trial in San Francisco Superior Court beyond this extension time. This case was at issue on May 23, 2022 and Plaintiffs did not act diligently in bringing this case to trial thereafter. The court notes that Plaintiffs also failed to act diligently in bringing this motion, which is set on improper notice given the method of service. However, in light of opposition on the merits, the ruling denying the motion, is also made on the merits of the motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOHN OWEN VS. CYNTHIA WALLACE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 18, 2022 line 3. PLAINTIFF JOHN OWEN MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED without prejudice to Mr. Cowan bringing the motion. This motion is filed by cross-defendant Owen seeking to quash service on cross-defendant Cowan. This is procedurally improper. While in the reply cross-defendant Cowan purports to be the moving party ("John Cowan's Motion to Quash Service of the Cross-Complaint filed by Wallace should be summarily granted"), the Notice of Motion and Motion are clearly brought by Owen. No authority is cited for the proposition that Owen may seek such relief. No special appearance made by Cowan to challenge the Court's jurisdiction due to purportedly improper service. The Court further notes that this motion was improperly noticed in department 302 in violation of the Local Rules and prior minute order issued by department 302 on May 24, 2022. In the future, all improperly noticed motions will be taken off calendar. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
